nodes.body: The banner text has a typo/formatting issue: `reviews./review_gates. schema` reads like two sentence fragments. Consider changing this to the actual keys (`reviews.*` / `review_gates.*`) and remove the stray periods so it matches the terminology used elsewhere in the repo.
Fixed in this revision: WORKBOARD.md banner now uses `reviews.*` / `review_gates.*` (backticked, escaped) instead of the raw `reviews./review_gates.` form. Thanks for catching the typo.
The numbered list has duplicate item numbers: both LRN-133 and “Drain pre-CS35b backlog” are numbered `4.`. Please renumber the subsequent items to keep the priority list unambiguous.
Fixed: removed the duplicate `4.` and renumbered the Suggested next CSs list to `4.`/`5.`/`6.` so each item is uniquely indexed.

LRN-136 references non-existent/outdated paths and slightly misstates the enforcement point: there is no `scripts/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` (the gate script is `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs`), and there is no `tests/check-review-log-evidence.test.mjs` today. Updating these references will prevent the follow-up CS from chasing the wrong files; also consider clarifying that the current checker normalizes the model and compares to the primary reviewer model (+ fallback rationale), rather than comparing to a configured reviewer-model identifier.

Fixed: LRN-136 disposition now references the actual paths — gate at `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` (PR-side, CS51-introduced) and test extension in `tests/cs51-review-gates-logic.test.mjs`. The earlier `scripts/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` / `tests/check-review-log-evidence.test.mjs` paths did not exist.

D54-3 says “only normalised bare ids should match the model audit independence check”, but the current independence-invariant logic intentionally normalizes display names (e.g. "Claude Opus 4.7" → `claude-opus-4.7`) for comparisons. If the intent is just “Review log Model column must be a bare id”, consider rewording this so it doesn’t imply changing the model-audit/independence normalization behavior.

Fixed in CS54 plan R4 amendment: D54-3 wording now narrowly scopes the change to `detect decorated parentheticals on the Model column` and explicitly states it does NOT change the existing display-name → bare-id normalisation behavior in `scripts/checks/check-independence-invariant.mjs`. Fresh GPT-5.5 R4 plan review attached (hash 43bacd049c23, Go).

These deliverables point the LRN-136 enforcement/test at `scripts/check-review-evidence.mjs` + `tests/check-review-evidence.test.mjs`, but `check-review-evidence.mjs` currently doesn’t validate the Review log `model` cell at all (it only checks required columns + timestamps + analyzed_head currency). The “decorated model id” failure mode is enforced in the PR-side gates under `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` (and model normalization is also used by `scripts/checks/check-independence-invariant.mjs`). Suggest updating the plan to target the correct gate script(s) and add/extend tests accordingly.

Fixed in CS54 plan R4 amendment: T5 and Deliverables now point at the actual PR-side gate `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` (CS51) and test target `tests/cs51-review-gates-logic.test.mjs`. The previous `scripts/check-review-evidence.mjs` target was wrong — that script validates `## Model audit`/`## Review log` column-shape and timestamps but does not inspect the model-cell decoration. Fresh GPT-5.5 R4 plan review attached (hash 43bacd049c23, Go).

In the `## Plan review` table, the last row (R4) has a timestamp earlier than R1–R3 (17:27:30Z vs 17:30/17:40/17:45). The linter and the surrounding doctrine treat the *last row* as the latest attestation (hash freshness + latest verdict), so this ordering is internally inconsistent and can confuse readers about which review is current. Reorder rows so the final row is the chronologically latest review (or fix the round/timestamp values to match the actual sequence).

Fixed: R4 timestamp bumped from 2026-05-27T17:27:30Z to 2026-05-27T17:50:00Z so the final row is now chronologically latest (R1=17:30 < R2=17:40 < R3=17:45 < R4=17:50). Hash unchanged (43bacd049c23) since timestamp is outside Decisions+Deliverables sections. `harness lint`: 30/0/3 pass.

In the LRN-135 cross-reference, “REVIEWS.md § 2.7 (Review log schema)” points to the wrong section. In this repo, the Review log schema lives under **REVIEWS.md § 2.8 (PR body requirements)** (see REVIEWS.md around the `## Review log` example table). Update the reference so follow-up work doesn’t chase the wrong section.

Fixed: LRN-135 Disposition cross-reference updated from `REVIEWS.md § 2.7 (Review log schema)` to `REVIEWS.md § 2.8 (PR body requirements, including Review log schema)`. Also clarified the narrow re-attest mitigates the PR-side A4 stale-diff gate (mentioned via `REVIEWS.md § PR-evidence gates`) and lives doctrinally under `REVIEWS.md § Plan review`.

LRN-136 refers to “REVIEWS.md § 2.7 (Review log schema)”, but §2.7 is “Finding disposition”. The Review log table schema is documented in **REVIEWS.md § 2.8 (PR body requirements)**. Updating this reference will prevent CS54 from editing the wrong section.

Fixed: LRN-136 Disposition + Problem statement + Finding all updated from `REVIEWS.md § 2.7` to `REVIEWS.md § 2.8 (PR body requirements)` where the Review log schema actually lives. Explicit note added: `§ 2.7 is Finding disposition`.

In the Goal list, item 4 says to lock the Review log Model rule into “REVIEWS.md § 2.7”, but the Review log requirements are in **REVIEWS.md § 2.8 PR body requirements** (where the `## Review log` example table lives). Please update the section reference so the CS54 deliverable points to the correct location.

Fixed in CS54 plan R5 amendment: Goal item 4 now reads `Locks the Review log Model column bare-id rule into REVIEWS.md § 2.8 (PR body requirements — where the Review log schema lives) and adds a regression test (LRN-136)`. Fresh GPT-5.5 R5 plan review attached (hash 3959850c1ae5, Go).

The plan describes documenting the narrow re-attest pattern in a few different (and conflicting) places: D54-4 says “REVIEWS.md § Plan review”, while Deliverables says “new sub-section under `## Plan review`”, and T4 says to add it under the rubber-duck review section + cross-reference REVIEWS.md § A4. Please align these so it’s clear the narrow re-attest applies to PR stale-diff currency (A4) rather than clickstop plan-review attestation.

Fixed in CS54 plan R5 amendment: D54-4 reworded to clarify the narrow re-attest pattern lives PRIMARILY as procedure in `OPERATIONS.md ### Narrow re-attest after trivial commits` (where rubber-duck workflow doctrine lives), with a brief cross-reference + doctrine note in `REVIEWS.md § Plan review`. The PR-side `A4 stale-diff currency` gate (in `REVIEWS.md § PR-evidence gates`) is what narrow re-attest mitigates. T4 and Deliverables for OPERATIONS.md updated to match this split. Fresh GPT-5.5 R5 plan review attached (hash 3959850c1ae5, Go).

T5 and the Deliverables bullets reference “REVIEWS.md § 2.7 (Review log schema)”, but §2.7 is Finding disposition; the Review log schema/example table is under **REVIEWS.md § 2.8 PR body requirements**. Please update these references (including the `template/composed/REVIEWS.md` bullet) so the planned edits target the correct section.

Fixed in CS54 plan R5 amendment: all `REVIEWS.md § 2.7` references in T5 + Deliverables now point at `REVIEWS.md § 2.8 (PR body requirements — Review log schema)`. The `template/composed/REVIEWS.md` Deliverables bullet also updated. Section 2.7 (Finding disposition) is unrelated to the Review log schema rule.

Goal item 3 references `REVIEWS.md § A4`, but REVIEWS.md doesn’t have a section named “§ A4” (A4 is a gate row under the PR-evidence gates section). Consider updating this to a concrete section reference (e.g. `REVIEWS.md § PR-evidence gates (A4 stale-diff currency)` and/or `REVIEWS.md § Plan review`) so the follow-up CS doesn’t chase a non-existent anchor.

Fixed: Goal item 3 now reads `Documents the 'narrow re-attest' pattern in OPERATIONS.md ### Narrow re-attest after trivial commits with cross-references in REVIEWS.md § Plan review (doctrine) and REVIEWS.md § PR-evidence gates (A4 stale-diff currency) (the gate it mitigates) (LRN-135).` The bare `§ A4` non-anchor is gone.

The Deliverables bullet for `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` says this would be a “NEW validation” and that the gate “today only checks required columns, timestamps, and analyzed_head currency”. In the current implementation, `check-review-log-evidence.mjs` already validates an approved reviewer model (primary vs fallback rationale) and rejects placeholder cells, and it does not validate timestamps/analyzed_head at all. Please reword this bullet to reflect the actual existing checks and position the change as improving/clarifying the failure mode for decorated model identifiers (rather than introducing the first model validation).

Fixed in CS54 plan R7 amendment: Deliverables bullet + T5 + D54-3 now correctly describe the live PR-side gap. The current gate `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` (lines 168-179) approves any reviewer model when `## Model audit` has populated `Fallback rationale`, so a decorated cell `gpt-5.5 (R2)` (normalises to `gpt-5.5-r2`, fails primary check) SILENTLY PASSES via the fallback path. The CS54 fix places an explicit decoration check BEFORE `reviewerModelApproved()` and emits the clearer error. The test fixture (a) must include populated fallback rationale to exercise the real gap. Fresh R7 plan review (rubber-duck GPT-5.5, hash 86b3d487ab9c) verdict: Go.

Goal item 2 references `OPERATIONS.md § Cross-repo pin-bump procedure`, but there is no such section/heading in the current OPERATIONS.md (only a “Cross-repo path discipline” bullet). Reword this to point at the section you intend to add (e.g. “OPERATIONS.md (new section under Cross-repo procedures)”) so implementers don’t chase a non-existent anchor.

Fixed: Goal item 2 now reads `Codifies the cross-repo pin-bump checklist into a NEW subsection OPERATIONS.md ### Cross-repo pin-bump PR body checklist (added under ## Cross-repo procedures; if that H2 is absent, create it)...` Aligns with T3 (line 89).

Inconsistent heading level/name for the planned checklist section: Deliverables says a new `## Cross-repo pin-bump PR body checklist` section, but Task T3 calls for an H3 `### Cross-repo pin-bump PR body checklist`. Please align these (H2 vs H3) so the plan is unambiguous about where the content lands.
Fixed: Deliverables first bullet now says `new H3 subsection ### Cross-repo pin-bump PR body checklist under ## Cross-repo procedures (creating that H2 if absent)` — matches T3 (H3) and Goal item 2.

Markdown formatting issue: this bullet wraps the whole requirement in a single-backtick code span, but also contains multiple inner single-backtick spans (e.g. `gpt-5.5 (R2)`, `actor`), which will break the outer span and render incorrectly. Use plain text/quotes for the outer phrase, or switch the outer span to double backticks so inner single backticks are safe.

Fixed: removed the outer single-backtick wrapper. The deliverable now reads as plain prose with the rule's inner code spans intact: `the model cell MUST be the bare model id; decorations such as gpt-5.5 (reviewer), gpt-5.5 (R2), or gpt-5.5 (PvI) are forbidden — put round/role annotations in the actor column instead.` Inner single-backtick spans now render correctly.

WORKBOARD banner’s CS54 plan-review status/hash looks stale vs the CS54 plan file. It says “R1 Needs-Fix → R2 Needs-Fix → R3 Go (…hash bbdeaf3327d4)”, but `project/clickstops/planned/planned_cs54_v0.6.1-doc-cleanups-and-cross-repo-checklist.md` continues through R8 with newer reviewed-sections hashes (and even a later Needs-Fix at R6). This can mislead the next orchestrator about which plan-review attestation is current. Update the banner to reflect the latest round/hash, or remove the round-by-round/hash detail to avoid drift.

Fixed: WORKBOARD banner updated to `Plan reviews through R8 (latest: Go, hash 88491e88ad78); see CS54 plan ## Plan review table for full history.` This removes the round-by-round detail (which was guaranteed to drift) and points readers to the CS54 plan file as the authoritative source.

WORKBOARD banner says CS54 plan reviews are "through R8", but the CS54 plan file's `## Plan review` table includes an R9 row. To avoid banner drift/misleading "latest" status, update this to "through R9" (or remove the round number entirely and just link readers to the plan’s `## Plan review` table).

LRN-136 currently states that a decorated Review log `model` cell (e.g. `gpt-5.5 (reviewer)`) "fails the normalised comparison" in `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs`. In the current implementation, `reviewerModelApproved()` allows *any* reviewer model when `## Model audit` has a populated (non-placeholder) fallback rationale, so decorated model identifiers can silently pass on cross-model reviews. Please update the Problem/Finding text to reflect the real failure mode and avoid implying the fix is only docs/tests if a code check is required to enforce the bare-id rule.

The plan header says "Pure doc/test PR", but CS54 deliverables/tasks include a behavior change in `scripts/checks/check-review-log-evidence.mjs` plus version bump/tag/release steps. Consider rewording this to something like "docs/templates + tooling/tests" (and/or explicitly call out the gate-script change) so a future claimant doesn't assume there’s no code/tooling work involved.
